data.title: Nike (NKE) to Cut 775 Jobs as Part of Automation Push
data.description:
Nike (NKE) is cutting 775 employees as the sneaker giant accelerates its use of manufacturing automation.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

These are the latest layoffs by Beaverton, Oregon-based Nike as it tries to boost its bottom line and share price. Last summer, the sports apparel maker announced 1,000 corporate job cuts. The new workforce reduction will mostly impact workers at Nike's distribution centers in Tennessee and Mississippi.

In a written statement sent to media, Nike said the latest layoffs are aimed at reducing "complexity, improve flexibility, and build a more responsive, resilient, responsible, and efficient operation." The company has repeatedly said that it plans to create a leaner workforce as part of its goal to get back to "long-term, profitable growth."

Nike's Turnaround Strategy

The layoffs come as Nike CEO Elliott Hill works to turn around the company after years of slowing sales and shrinking profit margins. The 775 job cuts also come as artificial intelligence and automation sweep across corporate America, impacting distribution centers and their workers. Last year, delivery giant UPS (UPS) announced plans to cut 48,000 roles as it adopts more automation at its warehouses.

"We're taking steps to strengthen and streamline our operations so we can move faster, operate with greater discipline, and better serve athletes and consumers," Nike said in the statement. "We are sharpening our supply chain footprint, accelerating the use of advanced technology and automation, and investing in the skills our teams need for the future."

The struggles came after its former top executive John Donahoe pursued a direct selling strategy that prioritized the retailer's stores and websites over wholesale partners.

As part of that strategy, Nike's distribution centers – and staff within those facilities – ballooned, but they don't have the volume to support those staffing levels, the people familiar with the matter said.

Under Hill, Nike has been working to woo back wholesale partners, clean out stale inventory and reignite innovation. When reporting fiscal second quarter earnings in December, Nike said its net income had fallen 32% as it contends with tariffs, costs associated with its turnaround and a slowdown in its key China market.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: 2026 F-Line E Gives Ford Stock (NYSE:F) Electric Semi Ambitions, No Help in Share Prices
data.description:
We all knew that legacy automaker Ford (F) had ambitions of getting into the electric vehicle market. It even realized these ambitions to some extent, before paring them down in the face of market reality. But some of those ambitions made a comeback, as Ford got in on the electric semi stakes with its new F-Line E truck. This did not bring much comfort to investors, though, who sent shares down fractionally in Monday afternoon's trading.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The F-Line E will hit roads in Western Europe this summer, reports note, and be available in a choice of four-by-two or six-by-two configurations. The F-Line E is rated for up to 26 tons, and will be powered by a set of four modular batteries. The batteries are nickel manganese cobalt (NMC) packs that offer around 98 kWh each. This gives the F-Line E a range of about 300 kilometers, or roughly, 185 miles.

Perhaps the best part here is charging speed. The batteries on the F-Line E can be recharged from 20% to 80% in about 45 minutes, which is about the time it takes to unload the truck. Plugging the truck in when unloading starts will have the truck mostly ready to turn around by the time it is emptied, reports note. If the truck needs to be reloaded, the chances the batteries will fully charge in that time only improve.

The Blue Light Special

K-Mart is pretty much a dead brand these days, ever since it was purchased by Sears some time ago. Some K-Mart locations remain, yes, but most of these are not even in the United States. This was a bit of a problem for a car service tech, who recently opened a car's hood to find a K-1 oil filter in place. This would not be such a problem, except K-1 oil filters were a K-Mart product.

The filter was found on a Ford vehicle, the engine compartment of which was found to be coated in a "…mass of black, caked-on gunk." But given that the engine in question was a 1964 Y-block that came out of a 1964 F-100 before being installed into a 1954 F-100, it is hard to blame the oil filter fully for failing here. Not even the service tech blames the filter; he noted that engine conditions of the era often meant sludge was a normal by-product.

Is Ford Stock a Good Buy Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on F stock based on two Buys, 11 Holds and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 30.64% rally in its share price over the past year, the average F price target of $13.87 per share implies 2.82% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: 'Worth a Shot, But…' Says Investor on Amazon Stock
data.description:
Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) wasn't one of the market's big AI winners last year, even as the AI revolution kicked into high gear and propelled many of the sector's heavyweights to new highs. Instead, the stock lagged the broader market, with gains of just 2% over the past year.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Ironically enough, the company's recent performance has been quite strong. Amazon generated $180.2 billion in revenue in Q3, up 12% year over year, while AWS posted a 20.2% increase in sales – its fastest growth in nearly three years. Meanwhile, the cloud provider reported a backlog of $200 billion, pointing to a sizable pipeline of revenue still to come.

Several factors weighed on the stock in 2025, including investor unease over heavy capital spending, a valuation that began the year near 40 times earnings, and broader concerns about the economic outlook.

So, could this be the year that AMZN's share price catches up to some of its hyperscaler peers? Investor Vladimir Dimitrov – and long-time AMZN bear – is changing his tune on the company.

"Amazon's underwhelming stock performance in recent years could change course in 2026," the 5-star investor opined.

While Dimitrov acknowledges the concerns about the circular AI financing deals sloshing around, the investor notes that the base case scenario in 2026 points to "robust" demand for cloud infrastructure and services. AWS's improving profitability over the past few years gives Dimitrov plenty of confidence that the company can succeed in taking advantage of this ongoing trend.

Still, the investor offers a major caveat or two for why an investment in Amazon may not pay off.

"The big unknown for AMZN share price in 2026 now remains the state of the economy and the equity market overall," adds Dimitrov.

Amazon isn't just a cloud provider, and its e-commerce segment could suffer if consumer confidence levels plummet in the year ahead, according to Dimitrov. Moreover, AMZN could be in bigger trouble than other equities if things go south, as it tends to be more volatile than the overall market.

"Either of these scenarios would most likely mean yet another year of underperformance for Amazon," the investor concludes.

All that being said, Dimitrov is willing to take the chance, rating AMZN a Buy. (To watch Vladimir Dimitrov's track record, click here)

Wall Street, meanwhile, is clearly optimistic. With 46 Buys and just 1 Hold, Amazon earns a Strong Buy consensus rating. The stock's average 12-month price target of $295.05 implies upside of 23% over the next 12 months. (See AMZN stock forecast)
data.author: Gabe Ross

data.title: ChatGPT and Claude Begin Referencing Elon Musk's Grokipedia in Some Responses
data.description:
Some responses generated by OpenAI's ChatGPT have recently included references to Grokipedia, an AI-generated encyclopedia created by Elon Musk's rival AI firm, xAI. However, these citations appeared only in a limited number of user queries, rather than broadly across the platform. Nevertheless, this behavior first came to light due to reporting by The Guardian, which noted that the references were unusual given that Grokipedia is a new and experimental source compared with more established knowledge bases. For context, Grokipedia launched in October as part of xAI's attempt to build an alternative to Wikipedia.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Unlike Wikipedia, which relies on human editors and moderators, Grokipedia is entirely AI-generated. In theory, this allows it to avoid human bias, though that assumption remains debated. It has also allowed the platform to grow extremely fast. In fact, Grokipedia has already produced 5,615,201 articles, which is nearly 79% of the size of English Wikipedia's 7,119,376 entries. In addition, The Guardian found that ChatGPT cited Grokipedia nine times while answering more than a dozen test questions.

Interestingly, though, these citations did not appear for well-known or widely documented topics, but instead showed up when users asked about more obscure historical or biographical claims. That pattern suggests selective use rather than a heavy reliance. The publication also noted that Anthropic's AI assistant Claude showed similar behavior. In response, an OpenAI spokesperson told The Guardian that ChatGPT draws from a wide range of sources while applying safety filters and clearly displaying citations. Meanwhile, Anthropic declined to comment, while xAI responded by saying, "Legacy media lies."

What Is the Prediction for TSLA Stock?

When it comes to Elon Musk's companies, most of them are privately held. However, retail investors can invest in his most popular company, Tesla (TSLA). Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 10 Buys, eight Holds, and seven Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average TSLA price target of $398.38 per share implies 8.8% downside risk.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Trump Considers Easing Federal Agent Presence in Minnesota
data.description:
Following mass public outcry over the shooting of 37-year-old Alex Pretti in Minneapolis over the weekend, President Trump is weighing whether to reduce the number of federal agents in the state if local officials work more closely with federal authorities.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The development comes after Trump spoke with Minnesota Governor Tim Walz over the phone on Monday, with both sides describing the call positively. "It was a very good call, and we, actually, seemed to be on a similar wavelength," Trump said in a Truth Social post.

Trump Sends 'Border Czar' to Minnesota

Earlier in the day, Trump said he would send "Border Czar" Tom Homan to Minnesota while announcing an investigation into welfare fraud within the state. An Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) veteran, Homan is seen favoring a more targeted enforcement approach rather than a broad strategy.

Meanwhile, Trump declined to take a stance on the shooting of Pretti, contrasting with other administration officials who said Pretti was at fault for bringing a gun to the protest. The case is currently being investigated by the FBI and the U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP).

data.title: AppLovin Stock (APP) Gets New Buy Rating & $700 Price Forecast as Axon Powers AI Advertising
data.description:
AppLovin (APP) is shaking off its rocky start to 2026 as it successfully expands beyond mobile gaming and into the massive e-commerce market. On Monday, shares climbed over 2% following a major upgrade from Needham & Company. Analysts now believe the company's AI-powered advertising engine, Axon, is winning over major brands like Etsy (ETSY) and Kalshi, positioning AppLovin as a serious challenger to the dominance of Google (GOOGL) and Meta (META).

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Axon AI Powers E-commerce Success

The secret behind AppLovin's recent success is its Axon 2.0 engine, a machine-learning tool that predicts which users are most likely to make a purchase. Originally built to help mobile gamers find new apps, the technology has proven surprisingly effective at selling physical goods. Needham analyst Bernie McTernan noted that "APP has already proven itself as a platform for mobile gaming advertisers," and the firm now expects the e-commerce segment to generate $1.45 billion in revenue this year.

The platform's growth is driven by its self-service model, which launched in late 2025. This allows even small businesses to set up campaigns without needing a massive marketing team. By using the Axon Pixel, brands can track exactly how many sales come from their ads, making the platform a tool for performance-driven marketers.

Major Brands Join the Ecosystem

AppLovin's client list is growing beyond its gaming roots. Analysts have spotted the Axon Pixel on over 435 websites, up significantly from just a few months ago. Recent additions appear to include the prediction market Kalshi and the global marketplace Etsy. These names join a growing roster of well-known retailers like Wayfair (W), Crocs (CROX), Yeti (YETI), and e.l.f. Beauty (ELF).

This shift is a direct threat to the "big three" of digital advertising: Google, Meta, and Amazon (AMZN). While AppLovin is still the underdog, its unique access to mobile users, reaching over 1 billion people daily, gives it a massive audience that traditional social media platforms might miss. Analysts are comparing this trajectory to the early days of TikTok, which disrupted the market by offering a completely new way for brands to find customers.

Shopify and Google Integration Simplifies Setup

The company has made it incredibly easy for stores to get started. Through a partnership with Shopify (SHOP), merchants can install the AppLovin "add-on" pretty easily. This automation handles the technical work of syncing product catalogs and setting up tracking codes.

This ease of use is a major selling point. Instead of spending weeks on technical setup, a brand can launch a campaign and start seeing results almost immediately. Needham believes this "low friction" approach will lead to a massive spike in user adoption throughout 2026. As more data flows through the Axon engine, the ads become even more accurate, creating a flywheel effect that makes the platform harder for competitors to beat.

Is AppLovin Stock a Buy, Hold, or Sell?

Turning to TipRanks, AppLovin stock (APP) has a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 18 Buys and two Holds assigned in the last three months. The average 12-month APP price target sits at $769.58, implying an upside potential of 44.5%.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Google's (GOOGL) YouTube Surpasses Reddit as Top Social Media Source for AI Models
data.description:
YouTube, the video-sharing platform owned by Google parent company Alphabet (GOOGL), has become the leading social media source for artificial intelligence models.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

According to a new report from Adweek, YouTube has surpassed Reddit (RDDT) as the primary social media source for AI large language models (LLMs). The report says that the transition marks a notable shift in how machines are receiving and digesting information.

Until now, Reddit had been the dominant social media source for AI models due to its text-heavy format. But Adweek says that YouTube's video content has now overtaken Reddit as the most frequently cited social media platform among AI-generated responses.

Video Replaces Text

As AI large language models become more powerful and sophisticated, they are able to increasingly process and consume video content as well as text. Previously, AI models had difficulty parsing the meaning of videos and other broadcast media.

Adweek says that transcripts, explainers, and other information associated with videos posted to YouTube have allowed the video platform to become a leading source for AI models. The report notes that YouTube appeared as a cited source in 16% of AI-generated answers over the last six months, compared with 10% for Reddit.

Is GOOGL Stock a Buy?

The stock of Alphabet has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 33 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 26 Buy and seven Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average GOOGL price target of $345.87 implies 3.51% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Tesla's (TSLA) Unsupervised Robotaxis Are Available, but Good Luck Getting a Ride
data.description:
Finding a Tesla (TSLA) Robotaxi without an in-car Safety Monitor is turning out to be harder than expected. Indeed, some riders who traveled to Austin, Texas, specifically to try these fully unsupervised vehicles, say that it's been extremely difficult to actually get a ride. While the EV maker recently removed Safety Monitors from a small portion of its Robotaxi fleet, most vehicles still operate with supervision. So, even though the technology is real, public access to it remains very limited.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Notably, David Moss, who is known for driving over 10,000 miles using Full Self-Driving v14 without intervention, spent nearly four days in Austin trying to hail a Robotaxi without a Safety Monitor. However, after 38 attempts, he still wasn't able to get one. The reason comes down to how Tesla is handling the rollout. In fact, the company has said that this is a controlled test, not a full launch.

More specifically, Ashok Elluswamy, who leads Tesla's AI program, explained that only a few unsupervised vehicles are currently mixed into a much larger Robotaxi fleet that still uses Safety Monitors. Over time, that ratio is expected to increase. Unsurprisingly, this cautious approach is designed to prioritize safety and reduce risk, which makes sense at this stage. Still, many are wondering how quickly Tesla can scale these fully unsupervised rides into a service that's meaningfully available.

What Is the Prediction for TSLA Stock?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 10 Buys, eight Holds, and seven Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average TSLA price target of $398.38 per share implies 9.1% downside risk.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Natural Gas Spikes 30% as Winter Storm Slams U.S.
data.description:
The price for a million British thermal units (MMBtu) of natural gas surged over 30% on Monday and has surpassed $6 for the first time since December 2022 as millions of Americans face the effects of Winter Storm Fern. With colder weather, demand for heating powered by natural gas increases, tightening supply and driving prices higher.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"These volatile price movements are directly tied to escalating heating needs; massive, expected storage withdrawals; and production setbacks from freeze-offs," said Price Futures Group senior market analyst Phil Flynn.

Gas Production Disruptions Meet Rising Heating Needs

The blistering weather has also affected natural gas production, with DNB Carnegie estimating 10% of U.S. production facing disruptions. At the same time, gas inventories were 4.8% higher compared to a year ago, which suggests that the price spike could be temporary, according to commodity strategists at ING.

Low temperatures are expected to persist this week, fueling continued demand for heating and keeping energy markets on edge.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Here's Why Iren Stock Is Trending Lower Today
data.description:
Shares in Iren Limited (IREN), the Sydney-based AI cloud capacity provider and rival to U.S.-based CoreWeave (CRWV), tumbled over 5% on Monday afternoon. This came after CoreWeave secured $2 billion in a new five-gigawatt capacity AI factory deal with Nvidia (NVDA), the AI chip leader and the world's most valuable company.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

What's the Deal About?

Under the arrangement, CoreWeave will build more than 5 gigawatts of power capacity by 2030 by developing and running AI factories on Nvidia's infrastructure, including its Rubin platform, Vera CPUs, and Bluefield‑based storage technology. In exchange, Nvidia will purchase CoreWeave's Class A common stock for $87.20 per share.

Iren Retreats as CoreWeave Wins Nvidia Boost

Reacting to the deal, analysts on Wall Street have hailed the extension of the collaboration between the two companies, with Raymond James calling it a "vote of confidence" in CoreWeave's AI cloud capacity.

In addition, DA Davidson analyst Alex Platt sees the deal as indicating robust demand for CoreWeave's computing capacity, while Needham argues that the arrangement is likely to come with future credit guarantees for CoreWeave to lease data center sites from third-party companies in addition to its own.

Wall Street Keeps Faith in Iren Stock

Iren, alongside Amsterdam-based Nebius (NBIS), has emerged as a key competitor to CoreWeave. The three companies are considered the favorites of hyperscalers or Big Tech companies seeking additional AI cloud computing capacity to boost their internal base.

Nonetheless, several analysts remain upbeat on Iren. For instance, H.C. Wainwright analyst Mike Colonnese believes the Australian company is well-positioned for a "transformative" year.

Is IREN Stock a Strong Buy?

On Wall Street, Iren Limited's shares continue to hold a Moderate Buy consensus rating. This is based on nine Buys, two Holds, and one Sell issued by 12 analysts over the past three months.

In addition, the average IREN price target of $82.80 suggests about 54% growth potential.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo
